seoTitle: How to Make 'TH' Small in Canva (Superscript)
metaDescription: Learn how to create superscript text in Canva, like making 'TH' small in ordinal numbers, for cleaner and more professional typography.
intro: Superscript formatting — like the small 'th' in '4th' — adds a professional touch to dates, ordinal numbers, and stylized text. Canva doesn't have a single dedicated superscript button, but you can recreate the effect using its text sizing tools. This guide shows how.

steps.heading: Type your full text first
steps.body: Enter the word or number as normal, for example '4th', before adjusting formatting.

steps.heading: Select just the letters to shrink
steps.body: Highlight only the 'TH' portion of the text you want to appear as superscript.

steps.heading: Reduce the font size of the selection
steps.body: Lower the font size of just the highlighted letters so they appear smaller than the rest of the text.

steps.heading: Raise the smaller text slightly
steps.body: If needed, split the text into a separate text box for the small letters and nudge it upward to align like a true superscript.

steps.heading: Check alignment and spacing
steps.body: Zoom in to confirm the smaller letters sit neatly next to the main number or word.

faq.question: Does Canva have a built-in superscript button?
faq.answer: Not a dedicated one — you achieve the effect manually by resizing and repositioning part of your text.

faq.question: Can I do this on Canva mobile?
faq.answer: Yes, though it's easier to control precise positioning on desktop where you have more editing space.

faq.question: Will this work with any font in Canva?
faq.answer: Yes, the technique works with any font since it's based on resizing text rather than a special character.

faq.question: Is there a faster way for repeated ordinal numbers?
faq.answer: Once you create one, you can copy and paste the formatted text and just change the digits.
